story.iWant: the TammaEngine to use IRoleBasedAgentResolver (interface) for phase-aware agent selection instead of a single hardcoded agent
story.soThat: different AI providers and configurations can be used for planning vs implementation phases while maintaining backward compatibility with existing single-agent setups

task.title: Make EngineContext.agent optional and add agentResolver

tasks.task: - [ ] Update `EngineContext` interface: make `agent` optional (`agent?: IAgentProvider`) - [ ] Add `agentResolver?: IRoleBasedAgentResolver` field to `EngineContext` (import type from `@tamma/providers`) - [ ] Update private field declaration: `private readonly agent: IAgentProvider | undefined` - [ ] Add private field: `private readonly agentResolver: IRoleBasedAgentResolver | undefined` - [ ] Add private field: `private readonly engineId = randomUUID()` (import from `node:crypto`) - [ ] Add constructor validation: throw `EngineError('Either agent or agentResolver must be provided in EngineContext')` if neither is provided - [ ] Add constructor WARN log when both `agent` and `agentResolver` are provided: "Both agent and agentResolver provided; resolver takes precedence for phase resolution" - [ ] Assign `this.agent = ctx.agent` and `this.agentResolver = ctx.agentResolver` in constructor

- [ ] Guard `initialize()`: only call `this.agent.isAvailable()` when `this.agent` exists - [ ] In `initialize()`: add warning when resolver mode is used: `this.logger.warn('Using resolver mode — ensure content sanitizer is configured in resolver options')` - [ ] Update `initialize()` log: use `this.config.agent?.model ?? 'resolver-mode'` for model field - [ ] Guard `dispose()`: only call `this.agent.dispose()` when `this.agent` exists - [ ] In `dispose()`: call `await this.agentResolver?.dispose()` to clear cached chains - [ ] Ensure `this.platform.dispose()` is always called in `dispose()`

- [ ] Add `private async getAgentForPhase(phase: WorkflowPhase): Promise<IAgentProvider>` method - [ ] When `this.agentResolver` exists, wrap call in try/catch: catch errors, log with `this.logger.error()`, and re-throw as `EngineError` - [ ] When no resolver, replace `this.agent!` non-null assertion with runtime guard: throw `EngineError('No agent available: neither agentResolver nor agent is configured')` if `this.agent` is falsy - [ ] Add `private getEngineTaskOverrides(): Partial<AgentTaskConfig>` method - [ ] Return empty object when `this.config.agent` is undefined - [ ] Return `{ model, maxBudgetUsd, allowedTools, permissionMode }` from `this.config.agent` when present - [ ] Note: `projectId` uses `config.github.owner/repo` (platform-specific); future work should use `this.platform.getProjectId()`

task.title: Update generatePlan() and implementCode() for resolver mode

tasks.task: - [ ] In `generatePlan()`: replace `this.agent.executeTask(...)` with resolver-aware pattern - [ ] Get agent via `await this.getAgentForPhase('PLAN_GENERATION')` - [ ] Pass engine overrides through resolver clamping: `this.agentResolver?.getTaskConfig('architect', this.getEngineTaskOverrides()) ?? {}` - [ ] Use explicit field allowlisting on resolver config: only pick `allowedTools`, `maxBudgetUsd`, `permissionMode` (no arbitrary fields) - [ ] Merge task config: `{ ...safeResolverConfig, prompt, cwd }` — engine always sets prompt and cwd - [ ] Wrap agent usage in try/finally with `await agent.dispose()` in finally block - [ ] Call `agent.executeTask(taskConfig, onProgress)` - [ ] In `implementCode()`: same pattern with `'CODE_GENERATION'` phase and `'implementer'` role - [ ] Pass engine overrides through resolver clamping: `this.agentResolver?.getTaskConfig('implementer', this.getEngineTaskOverrides()) ?? {}` - [ ] Wrap agent usage in try/finally with `await agent.dispose()` in finally block - [ ] Preserve all existing prompt construction, JSON parsing, cost tracking, and event recording

- [ ] Verify existing tests still pass with single `agent` (no `agentResolver`) - [ ] Test constructor throws with message `'Either agent or agentResolver must be provided in EngineContext'` when neither provided - [ ] Test constructor logs WARN when both `agent` and `agentResolver` provided - [ ] Test `initialize()` with `agentResolver` only: does not call `isAvailable()` - [ ] Test `initialize()` with both: still validates single agent's availability - [ ] Test `dispose()` with `agentResolver` only: does not call `agent.dispose()`, does not throw - [ ] Test `dispose()` calls `agentResolver.dispose()` to clear cached chains - [ ] Test `generatePlan()` uses `PLAN_GENERATION` phase, gets architect role from resolver - [ ] Test `generatePlan()` disposes agent after use (try/finally) - [ ] Test `implementCode()` uses `CODE_GENERATION` phase, gets implementer role from resolver - [ ] Test `implementCode()` disposes agent after use (try/finally) - [ ] Test task config merge: engine overrides passed through resolver's `getTaskConfig(role, overrides)` for clamping - [ ] Test task config uses explicit field allowlisting (only `allowedTools`, `maxBudgetUsd`, `permissionMode`) - [ ] Test `config.agent?.model ?? 'resolver-mode'` logged correctly in both modes - [ ] Test resolver error in `getAgentForPhase()` is caught, logged, and re-thrown as `EngineError` - [ ] Test fallback path throws descriptive `EngineError` instead of non-null assertion - [ ] Test events emitted during resolver mode include resolved agent/provider identity (not just 'unknown') - [ ] Test task config merge with `permissionMode: 'default'` (not just bypassPermissions) - [ ] Test mocks satisfy `IRoleBasedAgentResolver` interface directly (no `as unknown as RoleBasedAgentResolver` casts)

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: `EngineContext.agent` is optional (`agent?: IAgentProvider`) and `agentResolver?: IRoleBasedAgentResolver` is added (interface, not concrete class)
Constructor throws `EngineError('Either agent or agentResolver must be provided in EngineContext')` when neither is provided
`initialize()` skips `agent.isAvailable()` in resolver mode, logs `'resolver-mode'` for model, and warns about sanitizer configuration
`dispose()` only calls `agent.dispose()` when agent exists; calls `agentResolver.dispose()` when resolver exists; does not throw in resolver-only mode
`getAgentForPhase(phase)` resolves agent via `agentResolver.getAgentForPhase()` when resolver exists, with try/catch error handling; falls back with runtime guard (no non-null assertion)
`generatePlan()` uses `PLAN_GENERATION` phase, merges resolver config with engine overrides via clamping, uses field allowlisting, and disposes agent after use
`implementCode()` uses `CODE_GENERATION` phase, merges resolver config with engine overrides via clamping, uses field allowlisting, and disposes agent after use

Task config merge: engine overrides passed through resolver's `getTaskConfig(role, overrides)` for clamping; only `allowedTools`, `maxBudgetUsd`, `permissionMode` picked from result; prompt/cwd always engine-controlled
Existing tests pass unchanged (backward compatibility with single agent)

New tests cover resolver-only mode: initialize, dispose, generatePlan, implementCode, config merge, provider disposal, error handling, both-modes precedence, event audit identity, permissionMode variants
When both `agent` and `agentResolver` provided: resolver takes precedence for `getAgentForPhase()`; WARN logged; single agent still validated in `initialize()`

docs.doc: Story specification with design, code snippets, and verification criteria
Technical architecture (event sourcing, naming conventions, error handling)
Project coding standards, TypeScript strict mode, import order, async/await rules

code.file: EngineContext interface (line 33), constructor (line 89), initialize (line 101), dispose (line 114), generatePlan (line 406), implementCode (line 646) -- add resolver support
Add test suites for resolver-only mode: constructor validation, initialize guard, dispose guard, phase-aware resolution, config merge
RoleBasedAgentResolver class implementing IRoleBasedAgentResolver interface (Story 9-8 dependency) -- provides getAgentForPhase(), getTaskConfig(), and dispose()
WorkflowPhase type (Story 9-1 dependency) -- used by getAgentForPhase()

constraints.constraint: Existing single-agent EngineContext (with `agent` field, no `agentResolver`) must work without any changes to calling code
All existing engine.test.ts tests must pass without modification
All code must compile under TypeScript strict mode (strict: true, noImplicitAny, noImplicitReturns)
Files use kebab-case; interfaces use I-prefix; types use PascalCase; constants use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE
Task config merge must not mutate input objects -- always create new objects via spread
Providers are NOT pooled; each getAgentForPhase() call creates a fresh provider (subprocess-based providers are stateful); providers MUST be disposed after each phase usage via try/finally
Story 9-8 (RoleBasedAgentResolver) must be completed before this story

interface.name: EngineContext
interface.description: Dependencies injected into TammaEngine at construction time. Contains config, platform, agent (now optional), agentResolver (new, typed as IRoleBasedAgentResolver interface), logger, eventStore, onStateChange, approvalHandler

interface.name: IAgentProvider
interface.description: Agent provider interface with executeTask(), isAvailable(), dispose()

interface.name: IRoleBasedAgentResolver
interface.description: Interface for resolving agent providers per workflow phase using configured role-provider mappings. Methods: getAgentForPhase(phase, context), getTaskConfig(role, taskOverrides?), dispose(). Engine uses this interface (not the concrete RoleBasedAgentResolver class) for dependency inversion.

interface.name: TammaConfig
interface.description: Root configuration interface. Has required `agent: AgentConfig` and optional `agents?: AgentsConfig`

standards.standard: 80% line coverage, 75% branch coverage, 85% function coverage
TDD: write tests before or alongside implementation
Mock external dependencies; no real API calls in unit tests

locations.location: Extend existing test file with new describe blocks for resolver mode

ideas.idea: Test constructor with only `agent` -- should work as before
Test constructor with only `agentResolver` -- should work
Test constructor with both `agent` and `agentResolver` -- should work (resolver takes precedence for phase resolution), WARN logged
Test constructor with neither `agent` nor `agentResolver` -- should throw EngineError with message 'Either agent or agentResolver must be provided in EngineContext'
Test initialize() in resolver-only mode does not call isAvailable()
Test initialize() in resolver-only mode logs 'resolver-mode' as model
Test initialize() in single-agent mode still calls isAvailable() and logs actual model name
Test dispose() in resolver-only mode does not throw and does not call agent.dispose()
Test dispose() in resolver-only mode still calls platform.dispose()
Test generatePlan() in resolver mode calls getAgentForPhase('PLAN_GENERATION')
Test generatePlan() resolver mode calls getTaskConfig('architect') for resolver defaults
Test implementCode() in resolver mode calls getAgentForPhase('CODE_GENERATION')
Test implementCode() resolver mode calls getTaskConfig('implementer') for resolver defaults
Test task config merge: resolver provides allowedTools, engine overrides model, prompt always set by engine
Test task config merge: when no resolver config, engine overrides from config.agent are used alone
Test getEngineTaskOverrides() returns empty object when config.agent is undefined
Test full pipeline with resolver: processOneIssue() works end-to-end using resolver agents
Test resolver error in getAgentForPhase() is caught, logged, and re-thrown as EngineError
Test fallback path throws descriptive EngineError instead of non-null assertion crash
Test events emitted during resolver mode include resolved agent/provider identity (not just 'unknown')
Test task config merge with permissionMode: 'default' (not just bypassPermissions)
Test dispose() calls agentResolver.dispose() to clear cached chains
Test generatePlan() and implementCode() dispose agent after use (try/finally)
Test mock satisfies IRoleBasedAgentResolver interface directly (no `as unknown as RoleBasedAgentResolver` cast)
Test both-modes: when both agent and agentResolver provided, resolver takes precedence, WARN logged, single agent still validated
